steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank separates two complete thoughts: 'thousands of women joined the war effort as nurses' and 'Among them was Ann Bradford Stokes...' Each side is an independent clause, so the blank requires a sentence boundary — not a comma alone.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — nurses, among: A comma alone between two independent clauses creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — nurses among: No punctuation at all between two independent clauses creates a run-on. ✗
  • Choice C — nurses. Among: A period correctly ends the first independent clause and lets the second begin cleanly with 'Among them.' ✓
  • Choice D — nurses, and, among: Adding 'and' with two commas creates an awkward, ungrammatical structure — 'and among them was' would need no internal comma after 'and.' ✗
That confirms C is correct.
